{"query":"Japanese government comments Bank of Japan independence 10-year JGB yields spike June 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1644,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.centralbanking.com/central-banks/governance/accountability/7976354/japanese-government-stresses-importance-of-boj-independence","title":"Japanese government stresses importance of BoJ independence - Central Banking","content":"8 days ago — Japanese government stresses importance of BoJ independence. Ministers' comments come after 10-year JGB yields spike to near 30-year highs.Read more
Japanese government ministers today (July 10) stressed the importance of central bank independence for market confidence after bond yields shot to record highs.
Domestic media reported that Minoru Kiuchi, minister of state for economic and fiscal policy, had told a press conference the Bank of Japan’s independence must be respected when guiding monetary policy.

If you don’t have a Central Banking account, please register for a trial."},{"url":"https://www.oxfordeconomics.com/resource/why-we-expect-jgb-yields-to-stay-high/","title":"Why we expect JGB yields to stay high | Oxford Economics","content":"We’ve raised our end-2026 10-year Japanese government bond (JGB) yield forecast to 2.8% from 2.5%.
The prospect of higher inflation will keep yields elevated globally, and concerns about Japan’s fiscal expansion will also persist.
We now see the risks to our JGB forecast as more balanced, although a further increase still isn’t out of the question.
The 10-year JGB yield is around 2.7%, having risen sharply from 2.1% in March, fuelled by market concern about Japan’s more aggressive fiscal stance, in addition to the global repricing of yields driven by real rates and rising inflation expectations.
The recent slight retreat in the 10-year yield can be attributed to June’s supplementary budget being smaller than markets anticipated.
Additionally, the government has proposed issuing “bridging bonds” to increase strategic investment.
The policy direction should become clearer by around the end of June, and we may need to revise our forecasts accordingly.
The combination of the slow rise in the effective interest rate cost and strong tax revenue driven by inflation and solid growth means Takaichi can stick to her ambitious fiscal promises.
Even with our new yield forecast, we don’t think the government debt-to-GDP ratio will start to rise until mid-2027, and then only gradually, thanks to the large stock of JGBs issued at times of low yields.

The Bank of Japan building. TOKYO, May 12 (Reuters) – Japan’s 10-year government bond yield rose to a 29-year high on Tuesday ahead of an auction of the same-maturity debt, as market participants took the summary of opinions of the Bank of Japan’s April policy meeting as hawkish. Some BOJ policymakers in April saw the need to raise rates soon, with one signaling the chance of accelerating the pace of hikes if inflationary risks heightened, a summary of opinions at last month’s meeting showed on Tuesday. “The market took the overall tone of the summary hawkish, and it braced for an early interest rate hike,” said Yuki Kimura, a bond strategist at Okasan Securities. Japan’s Ministry of Finance will hold an auction of the 10-year bonds later in the day. The 30-year yield JP30YTN=JBTC inched up 2 bps to 3.78%.
